rulings.Case Title: IVA MCGREW et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 17, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Intervenors¿ (on behalf of Rich-Tex, Inc.) motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar. Rich-Tex, Inc. was dismissed April 14, 2014. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IVA MCGREW et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc., Fka Kaiser Cement Corporations
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 17, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the identification of the Kaiser Cement products is inadmissible. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order conforming to CRC 3.1362 and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IVA MCGREW et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 17, 2014 IN DEPARTMENT 503, LINE 1, Defendant Kelly-Moore Paint Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. McGrew was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability against defendant. Notwithstanding the defects of the written discovery that defendant propounded, plaintiffs¿ responses to special interrogatories and requests for production and the deposition testimony of Mr. Larson and Mr. Graham also preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, Mr. Larson¿s deposition testimony and declaration, Mr. Graham¿s deposition testimony, defendant¿s General Order No. 129 responses and the deposition testimony of Mr. Merrill would create a triable issue whether Mr. McGrew was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Paco joint compound) attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARY JO JONES AS SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST AND AS et al VS. COLLINS & AIKMAN PRODUCTS CO. et al

The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)
